files.latestApprovalRef: 2026-07-21 Dan explicit approval (poster ad quality harness session 4, S4/S6 — Dan asked to "fix S4/S6"). Poster copy is frozen into offer_versions at publish and never backfilled, so a business that renames leaves every already-published poster printing the OLD name. Observed live: a poster reading "Test Cafe" on a screen that said "The Colonel's Brew" three times around it, and it was WORSE in the feed, which suppresses the merchant line so the stale poster name was the only merchant identity shown. Added an optional merchantName prop: when the caller knows the live business name it is substituted for the frozen business_name before sanitizePosterCopy, so the live name still goes through the same length clamp and policy scan as a stored one. Omitting the prop uses the frozen value unchanged. Business name is IDENTITY, not an offer fact, so nothing about the locked offer facts changed. Verified live in pixels on the S10 (Metro serving the working tree): the feed poster now reads "The Colonel's Brew". Rebuild-gated (client renderer). Prior ref: 2026-07-21 Dan explicit approval (poster ad quality harness session 4, S2): the renderer half of the empty-hero fix. heroLine was posterText(copy.headline || copy.offer_line_2) and secondaryLine was posterText(copy.offer_line_2 || copy.headline) — each slot fell back to the other, so once the builder stopped duplicating, the renderer would have re-created the duplicate anyway. Both fallbacks removed in BOTH template variants (V1 and V2), and secondaryLine now returns "" when it equals the hero. That equality guard is deliberately renderer-side rather than builder-side: poster specs are frozen into offer_versions at publish and never backfilled, so for every es/ko poster already published with the duplicate baked in, the renderer is the only thing that can stop it printing twice for the rest of its run. Every poster line is absolutely positioned, so an empty hero leaves a gap and shifts nothing. No layout constant, colour, scrim, offer fact, or English output changed. Rebuild-gated (client renderer only). Prior ref: 2026-07-21 Dan chat approval (poster ad quality harness, session 2 — R1 light-background legibility): Dan approved lowering POSTER_SCRIM_TARGET_LUMA 0.20 -> 0.18 to lift bright-photo headline contrast out of the WARN band. A matched on-device A/B (s04, Korean over the luma-0.755 latte photo, same scroll, same crop) measured that constant change as +0.07 only — 4.01:1 -> 4.08:1 — because the constant sizes scrim alpha at y=0, where no text sits, and alpha saturates at 1 - target/luma (0.735 -> 0.762 on that image). The approved OUTCOME was delivered by fixing the scrim's REACH instead: the top-scrim LinearGradient previously fell to half alpha by 0.16 of canvas height and to zero by 0.30, while the headline occupies 0.110-0.236, leaving it under only ~0.33 effective alpha. Stops are now mid 0.5 -> 0.6 alpha and locations 0.16/0.30 -> 0.20/0.32, measuring 4.01:1 -> 4.99:1 on the same cell (kicker 3.53 -> 4.05, business 5.80 -> 6.16). POSTER_SCRIM_TARGET_LUMA is deliberately left at 0.20 so the geometry is the only moving part; its comment now documents that it governs y=0 only. No cell can regress — the scrim is only drawn when alpha > 0, which never happens for an image already darker than the target — verified on the no-luma 0.66 fallback path too (s06, scroll-matched: headline 8.64 -> 9.24, all blocks PASS). Copy, templates, text limits, offer facts and generation are unchanged. Client-only: rebuild-gated, no deploy impact.

files.latestApprovalRef: 2026-07-21 Dan explicit approval (poster ad quality harness session 4, S2 — Dan reviewed the recommendation and replied "i approve your reccomendation for S2"). Every non-English poster printed the SAME SENTENCE TWICE, in two type sizes: copyForLocale set headline := offer_line_2 for every locale except en-US, and nothing deduped, so the 72px hero and the line beneath it were identical ("AL COMPRAR 1 CUALQUIER" / "AL COMPRAR 1 CUALQUIER", and the Korean equivalent). Found on the shopper surfaces in session 4; no test covered the substitution. The approved shape was "hero = the reward", but implementing it revealed a hard constraint that changed the fix: validatePosterSpecV1 (posterAdSpec.ts:132-138) binds BOTH offer_line_1 and offer_line_2 to the deterministic lines from the locked offer definition, for EVERY locale — facts are authoritative — so the reward cannot be moved out of the gold slot and any headline drawn from the offer facts necessarily duplicates one of the two lines. With no translated creative headline available (the localization bundle is generated AFTER the poster spec is built), the only contract-safe answer is to leave the hero EMPTY for non-English locales: the offer is still stated in full by offer_line_1 + offer_line_2, and assertPosterCopyPolicy treats a missing headline as a WARNING, not a failure. Changes: (1) copyForLocale sets headline "" for non-en instead of copying offer_line_2; (2) buildPosterCopyByLanguage re-asserts the empty hero after sanitizePosterCopy, whose headline fallback is offer_line_1 and would otherwise have re-created the duplicate hero-vs-gold; (3) AdPosterCanvas hero no longer falls back to offer_line_2 and the secondary line no longer falls back to the headline, in BOTH the V1 and V2 template variants, plus a guard that suppresses the secondary when it equals the hero — that guard is what protects the specs ALREADY PUBLISHED with the duplicate baked in, since stored specs are never backfilled (FINDINGS S6). No offer fact, discount figure, mechanic, or publish behaviour changed; English output is untouched. Deploy impact: this file is imported by supabase/functions/ai-generate-ad-variants/index.ts, so new generations need an edge deploy and client rendering needs a rebuild. Prior ref: 2026-07-21 Dan standing approval (poster ad quality harness session 4, S1/S3 — the R9 residual that session 3 recorded as deliberately NOT addressed). Found on the SHOPPER surface, which had never been judged: rebuilding the live Colonel's Brew cookie deal with current code showed the es-US poster carried NO word saying anything was free. Cause: the BUY_ONE_GET_SOMETHING_FREE branch composed the line and THEN clamped it to 28 chars from the front. English states the offer with a PREFIX ("FREE <item>") so a front clamp only shortens the item; es-US and ko-KR state it with a SUFFIX ("<item> GRATIS", "<item> 무료") so the same clamp deletes the offer itself. "GALLETA DE TU ELECCIÓN GRATIS" is 29 chars against a 28 budget and lost GRATIS by ONE character; English cleared the same budget with one character to spare (27/28), which is why this was never seen — everything is judged in English. Fix: new composeOfferLine measures the locale template's affix cost first and fits the ITEM to what is left, so the fixed words can never be clamped away; new fitLocalizedItem adds a head-INITIAL fitter for es-US (drop a leading determiner, keep the longest fitting PREFIX, never end on a Spanish function word) because reusing fitItemLine's suffix bias was MEASURED to emit "DE TU ELECCIÓN GRATIS" — it kept GRATIS and destroyed the noun instead. en-US and ko-KR keep the existing suffix bias (both are head-final) and their output is byte-identical on all four measured cells. Also switched the two bare-item call sites from fitItemLine to fitLocalizedItem so a long Spanish item cannot produce a suffix fragment there either. Results: "GALLETA DE TU ELECCIÓN"/"AL COMPRAR 1 CUALQUIER" -> "GALLETA GRATIS"/"AL COMPRAR 1 BEBIDA DE CAFÉ"; "AL COMPRAR 1 SÁNDWICH DE" -> "AL COMPRAR 1 SÁNDWICH". No offer fact, discount figure, mechanic, layout, or publish behaviour changed — the Spanish lines now STATE the offer they previously deleted. Deploy impact: this file is imported by supabase/functions/ai-generate-ad-variants/index.ts (buildPosterSpecFromOfferDefinition), which builds the poster spec server-side at generation time, so newly generated posters need an EDGE DEPLOY and client rendering needs a REBUILD — NEITHER performed. NOT COMMITTED. Prior ref: 2026-07-21 Dan explicit approval (poster ad quality harness session 3, R7 - both causes, chosen from a 3-option prompt): posterItemLabel discarded the words that carry product identity. Cause 1 - "hot", "iced", "ice", "cold", "fresh" were listed in POSTER_ITEM_STOP_WORDS as droppable size modifiers, so "large cold brew" reduced to "brew" and a live poster read "BREW FOR LESS" at a shop called The Colonel's Brew. They are removed from that list; size words (large/medium/small/regular) stay droppable because they never identify a product. Cause 2 - the known-word branch returned the matched word ALONE regardless of the character budget, so "12 ounce bag of whole bean coffee" became "coffee" and "blueberry muffin" became "muffin", even though the headline limit is 28 and the label only needs to fit 19. New expandKnownItemWord walks BACKWARDS from the known word keeping preceding modifiers while they fit, stopping at a stop word or connector - which is what stops "cookie of your choice" reaching past "of" (head-FIRST phrase) and stops "any large coffee drink" picking up "large". posterHeadlineFallback now budgets each branch separately (28 minus its own suffix; the pair branch budgets each side to 9) so longer labels cannot overflow the pair and trade one fragment for another. Results: "cold brew", "whole bean coffee", "iced vanilla latte", "blueberry muffin". No offer fact, discount figure, mechanic, layout, or publish behaviour changed - only how much of the merchant's own item name survives into the deterministic headline, and strictly MORE of it survives. All 3 poster fixtures unchanged (COFFEE + COOKIE BREAK, LATTE BONUS, CROISSANT FOR LESS) and the R6 connector test still passes. One pre-existing expectation deliberately updated: "Large iced americano" now yields "ICED AMERICANO FOR LESS" instead of "AMERICANO FOR LESS" - size word still dropped, identity word now kept, which is the intended behaviour and is documented in the test. Deploy impact: this file is imported by supabase/functions/ai-generate-ad-variants/index.ts, so it needs an edge deploy and an app rebuild. New helper fitItemLine prefers the longest word-aligned SUFFIX that fits, then drops a leading stop/connector word so it can never emit "OF WHOLE BEAN COFFEE"; the same item now renders "BAG OF WHOLE BEAN COFFEE" (24 chars, complete). Traced every input to generateVerifiedAdLocalizationBundle: copy.*, offerDefinition, research, merchantProfile, sourceLocale - and even localizationImageAltText is built from businessName + copy.headline + offerLine, not from the produced image. The only image-derived field anywhere in reach was offerDefinition.sourceAssetIds, which is a pure passthrough: buildOfferDisclosureLine takes Pick<OfferDefinitionV1, 'canonicalTermsLine' | 'schedule' | 'perUserClaimLimit' | 'redemption'> so it structurally CANNOT read it, and no canonical line, disclosure id or hash derives from it. Change: sourceLocale and the offer definition are hoisted above the image stage as offerDefinitionFacts (built with sourceAssetIds: []), the localization call is started there as localizationPromise and awaited at its old site, and the post-image offerDefinition is now a SPREAD of offerDefinitionFacts with sourceAssetIds filled in - never a second build - so the object localization was verified against and the object used downstream cannot drift. localizationPromise?.catch(() => {}) attaches a rejection sink so an image-chain throw cannot orphan the promise into an unhandled rejection that kills the isolate. Verified nothing mutates copy, research or offerDefinition between the two stages, and that every hoisted identifier (offerContract, eligibilityInput, offerScheduleSummary, redemptionLimit, outputLanguage) is defined by line 3975, well above the image stage. Expected saving ~8s of ~44.6s, which also buys headroom against the ~150s WORKER_RESOURCE_LIMIT kill. Identical provider calls, identical output, identical spend; no prompt, image, poster-layout, copy, offer-fact, revision, or publish behaviour changed. stageTimingsMs.localization now overlaps stageTimingsMs.image by design and is documented as such in-code. Coverage: a new 'runs localization concurrently with the image chain, not after it' contract test in ai-generate-ad-variants-telemetry-source.test.ts pins the call ordering, the rejection sink, and the spread, so a future edit cannot silently re-serialize it. Validation: deno check, typecheck:functions, gate:ai-poster-lock, gate:ai-ad, copy:evaluate, typecheck, lint, npm test. Deploy impact: REQUIRES an ai-generate-ad-variants edge deploy to take effect; not deployed as of this entry. Prior ref: ALSO, and this was caught by reading the revision path rather than by a test: removing the kicker turned an existing HARD FAIL into a trap. checkRevisionCandidate pushed 'kicker_unchanged_for_subheadline_feedback' whenever intent.requiresKickerChange and the kicker had not changed - with the kicker now permanently empty that condition is always true, so ANY revision feedback mentioning subheadline/subline/supporting line/second line/smaller text would have hard-failed EVERY candidate. That hard fail and its soft score are removed, the deterministic-kicker backstop that fed it is removed, and the two now-dead helpers (revisionKickerChanged, deterministicRevisedKicker) are deleted. Subheadline feedback now routes to the card description, which is what the prompt says, and hasVisibleRevisionCopyChange still rejects a revision that changed nothing. 2026-07-21 Dan explicit approval (poster ad quality harness session 3, R12 - 'stop asking + stop generating', chosen from a 3-option prompt): the poster subheadline/kicker was UNRENDERABLE. copyForLocale (lib/poster/posterCopy.ts) ends with a hard `subline: undefined` for every locale, and AdPosterCanvas renders copy_by_language via posterCopyFromSpecForLocale - never spec.copy - so the eyebrow could never appear. Proved by building a real spec and inspecting it: base copy.subline = "LOCAL CAFE" while copy_by_language en-US/es-US/ko-KR were all undefined. Meanwhile the publish screen showed the merchant an editable TextInput with a live 32-char counter, seeded from the model's poster_kicker, under a comment promising the preview 'always shows exactly what will publish'. Merchants could type a subheadline, watch the counter, publish, and it appeared nowhere. The poster spec is now built with `subline: null` instead of `subline: copy.poster_kicker`, pinned so the kicker cannot return via a model that ignores the prompt. poster_kicker remains on the type and in the revision-tracking helpers for stored-spec compatibility; only the path into the poster spec is cut. SCOPE NOTE: this is the shallow removal - PosterCopyV1.subline, copyForLocale, checkMerchantPosterSubline and fixtures/ai-poster-copy-offers.json are deliberately UNTOUCHED so already-published poster specs and copy:evaluate stay valid; only the merchant-facing field and the generation of it are removed. No offer fact, discount figure, mechanic, layout, or publish behaviour changed. Deploy impact: edge deploy required for prompt.ts + index.ts; app rebuild required for ai.tsx. Prior ref: 2026-07-20 Dan standing approval (poster ad quality harness run 2, R5/R6 headline craft): live Tier-2 generations produced formulaic and broken poster headlines - three cells in one batch used the bare template <ITEM> SAVINGS (Loaded Nachos Savings / Acai Bowl Savings / Birria Tacos Savings), and a Haircut-and-fade deal produced AND FADE SAVINGS, an ungrammatical fragment that is NOT a length clamp (24 chars fits the 28-char limit) but a dropped head noun on a paid ad. Two pattern-level gates were added to posterHeadlineGateReasons, implemented as pure exported predicates in lib/ad-copy-style-gate.ts (startsWithDanglingConnector, isFormulaicValueHeadline) so they are unit-testable and reusable rather than inline regexes: POSTER_HEADLINE_DANGLING_CONNECTOR rejects a headline opening with and/or/but/plus (articles and prepositions are deliberately still allowed, and a word merely beginning with those letters - Andouille, Orange, Butter - is not matched), and POSTER_HEADLINE_FORMULAIC_VALUE rejects a headline ending in a bare value word (savings/deal/special/offer) preceded by at least one word, so a headline that merely contains the word is untouched. A rejected candidate takes the existing repair-or-deterministic-fallback path, exactly like the pre-existing POSTER_HEADLINE_OVER_LIMIT and POSTER_HEADLINE_TRY_OUR reasons. prompt.ts gains matching up-front guidance so the model avoids both shapes before the gate has to reject them. Deal facts, offer mechanics, image generation, poster layout, and publish behavior are unchanged. Per repo rule the fix is pattern-level - no item name is special-cased, and prompt.test.ts asserts the guidance does not name any observed item. Coverage: 18 predicate unit tests in lib/ad-copy-style-gate.test.ts incl. false-positive guards, prompt regression assertions, copy:evaluate green. files.latestApprovalRef: 2026-07-21 Dan standing approval (poster ad quality harness run 2, §2 locked-file scope) — Tier-2 image-prompt craft: the poster prints its 72px headline over the top quarter and its offer block over the bottom third, but measured across the corpus the TOP band came back the BUSIEST part of the photograph in 5 of 6 cells (mean row-wise luminance sd 0.26 top vs 0.23 middle). buildGeminiAdImagePrompt already asked for calm overlay zones, but only in the abstract. Replaced that bullet with geometry the model can act on: the fractions the app prints over, the hero subject's lane (25-65% of height, out of both bands), the top quarter as ONE continuous softly defocused backdrop, the named high-contrast offenders to keep out of it (windows, lamps, bright highlights, shelves, doorways, hard edges), and the same rule for the bottom third. The P1 full-bleed wording and its 'never empty bands' guard are retained verbatim. Matched regen (same offer facts, same business context, same cell ids m03/m04/m06 — only the prompt changed) measured mean top-band busy-ness 0.301 -> 0.223, -26%, with the inversion fixed in 2 of 3; m04 still composed a busy interior, so this is an improvement, not a guarantee, and the adaptive scrim remains the backstop. All three stayed native 4:5 928x1152, full bleed, correct subject, text-free, no fact drift. No provider order, models, text-free/no-QR rules, genericizeItems path, copy, templates or text limits changed. Edge-function change: DEPLOYED to prod ai-generate-ad-variants 2026-07-21. Prior ref: 2026-07-21 Dan chat approval ('Bump all 3 hashes'): ratifies commit 3d4caa80, whose lock-manifest bump was missed (the preceding commit a8e414fe did bump it). poster.luma computed null in every prod response, so the native renderer fell back to its fixed 0.66 scrim instead of an image-sized one. The band math is split out as the pure, decoder-free computeBandLuminanceFromRgba(data, width, height); normalizeGeminiImageToPng already holds decoded RGBA on the JPEG->PNG path, so it measures luminance there and returns it, and luma rides GeminiImageResult out through the retry chain — the dominant path pays for no second decode and never calls pngjs sync.read (the suspected null cause). New computeImageBandLuminanceDetailed returns {luma, decoder, reason} with short self-authored reason codes (empty_bytes, png_sync_read_unavailable, decode_threw:<Name>) that never carry an upstream provider body; computeImageBandLuminance is kept as a thin wrapper so existing callers are unchanged. No copy, offer, prompt, template, or QA logic changed. Edge-function change: requires a Supabase deploy to take effect. Prior ref: 2026-07-20 Dan chat approval (poster-ad quality harness, P1 full-bleed + luma helper): (P1) the 4:5 image prompt now demands the photo fill the whole frame edge to edge — the old 'leave clean visual space near the top/bottom' line made the model pad two-item/BOGO compositions with flat letterbox bands (confirmed on i01/i05); it is replaced with 'fill edge to edge, no borders/letterboxing/flat bands, extend the scene to every edge even for two items' plus 'top/bottom zones stay part of the photo but calm/low-contrast for overlay'. (luma) added exported computeImageBandLuminance(bytes, mime) — a fail-safe pngjs/jpeg-js decode that returns top/bottom band relative luminance (0..1) for the renderer's luminance-aware scrim; returns null on any error. No provider order, models, text-free/no-QR rules, or genericizeItems path changed. Prior ref: 2026-07-20 Dan chat approval ('deploy my fix now') — F4 image-refusal fix: buildGeminiAdImagePrompt gains a genericizeItems option that drops evocative brand tokens (business name, item names, AI concept line) and asks for the business-category product, used as the fallback prompt after a provider content-refusal; all text-free/no-QR/style rules and the branded default path are unchanged. Prior ref: 2026-07-18 Dan chat approval ('do you need to complete item 4 and item 6? ... I approve you to do whatever you need'): item 4 — Gemini 3 Interactions image requests now natively request the output shape via the currently documented response_format field ({type:'image', aspect_ratio, image_size}) so 4:5 poster images are generated at 4:5 instead of relying on prompt prose and client cover-cropping. Safety net for the 2026-07-14 BAD_REQUEST incident class: if the endpoint rejects the request with HTTP 400, the call logs gemini_image_config/response_format_rejected and retries once without response_format, so a Google contract change can never break image generation. Prompts, models, legacy generateContent imageConfig, fallback order, and telemetry shape unchanged. (2026-07-17: a briefly staged no-OpenAI-fallback change was reverted un-deployed at Dan's direction.)
